21.correct: The Constitution of India originally had 8 Schedules when it was adopted in 1949.

wrong.A: 7 is one fewer than the original number.
wrong.D: There are now 12 schedules, but originally there were 8.

21.fact: Schedules 9, 10, 11 and 12 were added by later amendments.

22.correct: The 92nd Amendment (2003) added four languages to the Eighth Schedule - Maithili, Bodo, Santhali and Dogri - so 'all of the above'.

wrong.A: Maithili was added but it was not the only one.
wrong.B: Bodo was added but not alone.
wrong.C: Santhali was added but not alone.

22.fact: The Eighth Schedule now lists 22 scheduled languages.

23.correct: Giddha is generally performed at weddings and harvest festivals.

wrong.B: It is not limited to religious ceremonies.
wrong.C: It is not for political gatherings.
wrong.D: It is a folk dance, not a classical dance competition.

23.fact: Giddha is the women's folk dance of Punjab, full of energy and humour.

24.correct: Henry Cavendish experimentally determined the value of the universal gravitational constant G (the 'Cavendish experiment').

wrong.A: Isaac Newton gave the law of gravitation but did not measure G.
wrong.B: Albert Einstein gave the theory of general relativity.
wrong.D: Johannes Kepler gave the laws of planetary motion.

24.fact: Cavendish's 1798 experiment used a torsion balance to 'weigh the Earth'.

25.correct: William Dalrymple's book 'The Last Mughal' describes the Indian Revolt of 1857 and the fall of Bahadur Shah Zafar.

wrong.A: It is not about the Partition of India.
wrong.C: It is not about the founding of the INC.
wrong.D: It is not a general account of the freedom movement.

25.fact: The book focuses on Delhi during 1857 and the last Mughal emperor.

26.correct: In senior men's kabaddi, an official match lasts 40 minutes - two halves of 20 minutes with a 5-minute break.

wrong.A: 30 minutes (two 15-minute halves) is the women's format.
wrong.C: 50 minutes of continuous play is incorrect.

26.fact: Kabaddi is the national game of Bangladesh and very popular across South Asia.

27.correct: During the Fourth Five-Year Plan (1969-74), a central industrial objective was self-reliance through strengthening heavy industry and reducing dependence on foreign aid.

wrong.A: It was not limited to import substitution in consumer goods only.
wrong.B: It was not about liberalizing foreign trade.
wrong.C: IT/services were not prioritised in that era.

27.fact: The Fourth Plan's themes were 'growth with stability' and self-reliance.

28.correct: In angiosperms (flowering plants), the reproductive parts are located in the flower.

wrong.B: The root absorbs water and anchors the plant.
wrong.D: The stem provides support and transport.

28.fact: The flower contains the stamen (male) and the carpel/pistil (female) reproductive organs.

29.correct: In tennis, 'deuce' means the score is tied at 40-40.

wrong.A: It does not mean the server has the advantage.
wrong.B: It does not mean the receiver has the advantage.
wrong.D: It specifically refers to 40-40, not just any tie.

29.fact: From deuce, a player must win two points in a row (advantage then game).

30.correct: In kabaddi, a bonus point is earned mainly by the raider crossing the bonus line with at least one foot in the air (and other conditions met).

wrong.A: Touching the baulk line alone does not earn a bonus.
wrong.B: Touching two defenders is not the bonus condition.
wrong.D: Avoiding a tackle is not the bonus rule.

30.fact: The bonus line gives raiders an extra way to score when enough defenders are present.

31.correct: The Damodar Valley Corporation (DVC) was established for multipurpose river-valley development (flood control, irrigation, power).

wrong.A: It was not set up for trade promotion.
wrong.B: It was not for steel production.
wrong.D: It was not for rural credit.

31.fact: The DVC (1948) was India's first multipurpose river-valley project, modelled on the TVA of the USA.

32.correct: The Pradhan Mantri Shram Yogi Maan-dhan (PM-SYM) scheme is designed for workers in the unorganised sector, providing a pension after age 60.

wrong.A: It is not specifically for housewives.
wrong.B: It is not for corporate-sector professionals.
wrong.D: It is not for government employees.

32.fact: PM-SYM is a voluntary, contributory pension scheme for unorganised workers.

33.correct: The Directorate General of Foreign Trade (DGFT) is responsible for formulating and implementing India's Foreign Trade Policy.

wrong.A: The RBI handles monetary policy and forex management.
wrong.B: NITI Aayog is a policy think-tank.
wrong.D: The Ministry of Home Affairs is unrelated to trade policy.

33.fact: The DGFT functions under the Ministry of Commerce and Industry.

34.correct: As of November 2024, about 64% of all PDS beneficiaries in India had completed their e-KYC.
34.fact: e-KYC helps remove duplicate/ineligible ration cards from the Public Distribution System.

35.correct: 'Mrichchhakatika' (The Little Clay Cart) is a play by Shudraka, NOT by Kalidasa.
35.fact: Kalidasa is celebrated as one of the greatest poets and playwrights of classical Sanskrit literature.

36.correct: Under the new policy, students who fail the year-end exam in Classes 5 and 8 are given additional coaching and a chance to re-appear (re-examination).

wrong.A: They are not transferred to another school.
wrong.B: They are not exempted from future exams.
wrong.D: They are not directly promoted to the next class.

36.fact: This reverses the earlier automatic 'no-detention' promotion up to Class 8.

37.correct: The Space Commission (and the Department of Space) was established in India in 1972.

wrong.A: 1961 is when INCOSPAR was set up.

37.fact: The Space Commission formulates policy; ISRO executes the space programme.

38.correct: The Muslim League called for 'Direct Action Day' on 16 August 1946 to press its demand for Pakistan.

wrong.A: 8 August 1942 was the Quit India resolution.

38.fact: Direct Action Day led to large-scale communal riots, especially in Calcutta.

39.correct: Per the PLFS 2023-24 annual report, the literacy rate among Muslims (aged 7 and above) was about 79.5%.

wrong.A: 58.5% understates the reported Muslim literacy rate.
wrong.C: 73.5% is lower than the figure cited.

39.fact: The Periodic Labour Force Survey (PLFS) is conducted by the National Statistical Office.

40.correct: In South Asia, Bangladesh topped the Global Gender Gap Index 2025.

wrong.B: Bhutan did not top the region.
wrong.D: Maldives did not top the regional ranking.

40.fact: The Global Gender Gap Index is published by the World Economic Forum.
